channel.title: NYT > Arts > Television
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Bait’ Review: Bond. Shahjehan Bond.
item.description: Riz Ahmed imagines the dark side of being a 007 candidate (a position he knows something about) in his Prime Video comedy series.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale

item.title: The ‘Daredevil: Born Again’ Showrunner Feels Your Pain, Really
item.description: That big death at the start of Season 1 was hard for Dario Scardapane, too. Season 2 might not be any easier.
item.dc:creator: Connie Chang
item.media:credit: Tommy Rizzoli for The New York Times
item.media:description: “It’s been a lot more satisfying to start from whole cloth in Season 2,” said Dario Scardapane, who had to take over “Daredevil: Born Again” midway through production of Season 1.

item.title: How ‘One Piece’ Fixed a Common Problem for Anime Adaptations
item.description: The Netflix hit uses a combination of craft and collaboration to tackle one of the trickiest challenges for live-action anime adaptations: wigs.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Motamayor

item.title: For ‘Buffy’ Fans, Nicholas Brendon’s Xander Was a Complicated Everyman
item.description: The actor, who died on Friday, was a fan favorite on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” even as his character mixed quiet heroism with hostility toward the show’s women.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.media:description: Nicholas Brendon, bottom left, as Xander Harris in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer.” His castmates included Alyson Hannigan, top left, Charisma Carpenter, right, and Sarah Michelle Gellar, center.

item.title: At ‘Baywatch’ Tryouts, Hoping to Be the Next Pam Anderson or Jason Momoa
item.description: Buff, waxed or otherwise, thousands answered an open casting call to become the next “Baywatch” discovery — or just “Drowning Swimmer No. 2.”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Sinna Nasseri for The New York Times

item.title: Taylor Frankie Paul and the Downfall of the ‘Bachelor’ Empire
item.description: As competing dating shows lean into modern norms, the ABC franchise finds itself mired in scandals and stymied by its traditional approach to love.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu and Shivani Gonzalez

item.title: A Review of the U.K.’s ‘S.N.L.’ Debut From Both Sides of the Atlantic
item.description: In a country with little knowledge of the American TV institution, the show landed in a new light. An American and a British critic found plenty to enjoy.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman and Gabriel Tate

item.title: Choose Your Netflix Mystery: Agatha Christie or Jo Nesbo
item.description: New international series offer British sleuthing, Nordic noir, anime heartbreak and the ethereal landscapes of China’s west.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Review: Valerie Cherish vs. the Machine
item.description: In its third season, the HBO satire grows from cringe-comedy character study into a dark satire of labor in the A.I. era.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik

item.title: Josh Johnson Ridicules the Army’s Opening to Older Recruits
item.description: “Look, according to People magazine — and apparently the U.S. military — 42 is the new 35,” Johnson said on Tuesday’s “Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Late Night Questions the ‘Very Big Present’ Trump Got From Iran
item.description: Josh Johnson was puzzled by what kind of gift the president could have received “from the people you are currently at war with.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Mel Schilling, Dating Expert on ‘Married at First Sight,’ Dies at 54
item.description: Ms. Schilling, an Australian psychologist, was diagnosed with colon cancer in 2023. Two weeks ago she said the disease had spread to her brain.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: Mel Schilling at the 2025 BAFTA Awards.

item.title: Comedy’s Most Manipulative Shot … and Its Greatest Weapon
item.description: Audience reactions are a staple of standup specials. But they’re a strange device when you take a closer look.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

item.title: Late Night Doesn’t Feel So Hot About ICE at the Airport
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump had “found a way to make the airport even worse.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: John Lithgow as Roald Dahl in ‘Giant’: A Study in Monstrosity
item.description: In Mark Rosenblatt’s play, a powerful portrayal of the beloved children’s book author who almost gleefully exposes his bigotry.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.media:description: John Lithgow in “Giant” at the Music Box Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Valerie Perrine, Screen Siren Who Won Critical Acclaim, Dies at 82
item.description: Known early on for skin-baring temptress roles, she later earned rave reviews, a Cannes award and an Oscar nomination for her performance in the Lenny Bruce biopic “Lenny.”
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams
item.media:credit: Chic Donchin/American Broadcasting Companies, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Valerie Perrine in “Malibu,” a 1983 television movie on ABC. “Unlike most sex symbols of the past,” one critic wrote, she “has almost always been singled out by the critics for her acting rather than her anatomy.”

item.title: Bill Cosby Loses Sex Assault Lawsuit and Faces a $59 Million Judgment
item.description: A California jury found that Mr. Cosby had abused Donna Motsinger in 1972 after inviting her to attend one of his comedy shows.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley and Rachel Parsons
item.media:description: Bill Cosby was accused in the civil suit of sexually assaulting Donna Motsinger in 1972 when he was a celebrated entertainer and she was a waitress in a restaurant he visited.

item.title: Lilias Folan, Who Brought Yoga to Middle America, Dies at 90
item.description: With her bright leotards and soothing, welcoming tone, she helped to demystify a discipline that many Americans in the 1970s viewed as a counterculture practice.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman
item.media:credit: Duane Howell/The Denver Post, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Ms. Folan in 1976. “I’ve always known I was put on this planet to do this sharing and this journey,” she said.

item.title: ‘Real Housewives’ Has Been a Meme Gold Mine for 20 Years
item.description: The Bravo TV empire, which turns 20 this month, has also been a gold mine for the internet.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: The Real Housewives of Orange County (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Elizabeth Renstrom for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen,’ Plus 6 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new horror thriller premieres and Miley Cyrus celebrates the 20th anniversary of “Hannah Montana.”
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:description: Adam DiMarco and Camila Morrone in “Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen.”

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3 Premiere Recap: The Old Razzle-Dazzle
item.description: The HBO comedy returned for a new season on Sunday, more than 11 years after the previous one.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.media:description: Lisa Kudrow is back for a new season of “The Comeback” on HBO.

item.title: Kendra Duggar, Wife of ‘19 Kids and Counting’ Star, Faces Child Endangerment Charges
item.description: Ms. Duggar was arrested two days after Joseph Duggar, a onetime star of the reality TV series “19 Kids and Counting,” was arrested in a sex abuse case. She appeared in the “Counting On” spinoff.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.media:description: Kendra Duggar was released from the Washington County Detention Center in Fayetteville, Ark., on Friday.

item.title: After ‘Baby Reindeer,’ Richard Gadd Confronts Male Sexual Repression
item.description: The writer and actor found unexpected success by sharing his trauma. Now he’s exploring male pain in a new way.
item.dc:creator: David Marchese
item.media:credit: Philip Gay for The New York Times

item.title: Scott Evans Recommends These Books to the Women in His Life
item.description: The “Access Hollywood” and “House Guest” host shares his love of “Jesus Christ Superstar,” Keke Palmer and celebrity memoirs that go deep.
item.dc:creator: Leigh-Ann Jackson

item.title: Nicholas Brendon, Beloved Sidekick on ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer,’ Dies at 54
item.description: He played the part of Xander Harris, one of Buffy’s closest friends, on the hit television show about a teenage girl who protects the world from monsters.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Hannah Ziegler
item.media:description: Nicholas Brendon, who played the lovable sidekick on the hit show “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” has died.

item.title: ‘The Bachelorette’ Took a Risk on Taylor Frankie Paul. It Backfired.
item.description: Ms. Paul’s built-in fan base and viral interpersonal drama presented an opportunity for the long-running reality TV show, until video of a past assault derailed the season.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and John Koblin
item.category: Mormons (Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints)
The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: Chuck Norris: A Life in Pictures
item.description: The actor, known for his skills as a martial artist, often played the warrior onscreen.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: ‘Monte Cristo’ Review: A Tale of Betrayal, on Two Counts
item.description: An adaptation has a twist that doesn’t track, and songs that benefit from an excellent cast, including Norm Lewis, Sierra Boggess and Adam Jacobs.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli

item.title: Two Revivals, ‘Tru’ and ‘The Fever,’ Tackle the Contagion of the Rich
item.description: Two monologue revivals — Jesse Tyler Ferguson as Truman Capote and Wallace Shawn’s solo — reveal how wealth warps our perceptions. Only one pays dividends.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

item.title: ‘Saturday Night Live’ Arrives in London
item.description: Britain has a storied history of sketch comedy, but its humor has a very different flavor than the American variety.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:description: The cast of the British version of “Saturday Night Live,” which premieres this weekend and will be available on Peacock in the United States.

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2, Episode 11 Recap: Chilling Effect
item.description: An unwelcome visit by some rough Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents added more complications to an already stressful day.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins
item.media:description: From left, Shabana Azeez, Josell Mariano, Fiona Dourif, JuJu Alexander, Noah Wyle and Ramona DuBarry in a scene from Episode 11 of Season 2 of “The Pitt.”

item.title: What To Know About the BTS Comeback Concert Streaming Live on Netflix
item.description: The K-pop supergroup’s first show in over three years will stream live on Netflix at 7 a.m. Eastern time on Saturday. The New York Times will cover it live from Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul on Saturday, hours before the BTS comeback concert.

item.title: ‘Jury Duty’ Returns. Can Prank Lightning Strike Twice?
item.description: The Amazon comedy, which surrounds an unwitting protagonist with professional actors, returns with a new season that takes place at a company retreat.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski
item.category: Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat (TV Program)

item.title: Late Night Pans Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
item.description: “Let me tell you: There is no doubt in my mind that everything he knows about Pearl Harbor begins and ends with a movie starring Ben Affleck,” Jimmy Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Bachelorette’ Season With Taylor Frankie Paul Canceled After Assault Video
item.description: Taylor Frankie Paul, who was set to star in the TV show, had pleaded guilty to aggravated assault after an encounter with her partner in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and John Koblin
item.media:description: Taylor Frankie Paul, right, in a preview for her season of “The Bachelorette.”

item.title: ‘Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man’ Review: Cillian Murphy Returns as Tommy Shelby
item.description: Cillian Murphy rides to the rescue of family and homeland in this affectionate capper to the popular television series.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis
